description:
You are the mobile build engineer for a small team.  
A colleague has asked you to automate a very small reporting task that is currently done by hand.  
A few “build-drop” directories already exist and contain Android APK and iOS IPA artifacts that follow one very rigid naming convention:

    app-release_<version>.apk     (Android)
    app-release_<version>.ipa     (iOS)

where <version> is always a dotted semantic version such as 1.2.3.

Your job:

1. Write a POSIX-compatible Bash script called
      /home/user/mobile_ci/tools/generate_build_report.sh  

   The script must do the following (in exactly this order):
   a. Count how many *.apk files exist under /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/android  
   b. Determine the highest (latest) version number that appears in the *.apk file names there.  
   c. Count how many *.ipa files exist under /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/ios  
   d. Determine the highest (latest) version number that appears in the *.ipa file names there.  
   e. Produce a one-line CSV report with the precise header and data format shown below and write it to
      /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/build_report.csv (the directory already exists and is writable).  
   f. Echo the very same two data lines (header + data) to STDOUT.

2. Run the script once so that the CSV file and the console output are created.

CSV format (the automated checker will compare byte-for-byte):

    platform,total_builds,latest_version
    android,<APK_COUNT>,<LATEST_APK_VERSION>
    ios,<IPA_COUNT>,<LATEST_IPA_VERSION>

Absolutely nothing else (no spaces before/after commas, no extra blank lines, no terminal colours, no carriage returns).

Constraints & helpful notes:
• Only basic shell utilities (ls, find, sort, wc, cut, awk, sed, tr, etc.) are allowed; assume GNU coreutils.  
• The script must be executable.  
• The script must work if additional APK or IPA files following the same naming convention are added later.  
• Do NOT hard-code the numbers or versions; compute them dynamically every time the script runs.  
• Keep the implementation simple—about 3-4 key command lines is enough.  
• You are free to create temporary variables or files inside /tmp but the final report path is fixed.  
• After you finish, /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/build_report.csv must contain the correctly formatted two-line report, and the same two lines must have appeared in your terminal output during the run.

Example of *correct* CSV content if there were 3 APKs (latest 1.2.3) and 4 IPAs (latest 1.2.5):

    platform,total_builds,latest_version
    android,3,1.2.3
    ios,4,1.2.5

Remember: write the script, make it executable, run it once, and verify that the CSV file now contains the expected data.
truth:
Initial filesystem state that the autograder will prepare before the agent starts:

1. Directories (all 755 unless otherwise specified):
   /home/user/mobile_ci
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/android
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/ios
   /home/user/mobile_ci/tools
   /home/user/mobile_ci/reports        (exists, 755, empty)

2. Files (all 644):
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/android/app-release_1.2.1.apk   (empty placeholder file)
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/android/app-release_1.2.2.apk   (empty placeholder file)
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/android/app-release_1.2.3.apk   (empty placeholder file)

   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/ios/app-release_1.2.1.ipa       (empty placeholder file)
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/ios/app-release_1.2.2.ipa       (empty placeholder file)
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/ios/app-release_1.2.3.ipa       (empty placeholder file)
   /home/user/mobile_ci/builds/ios/app-release_1.2.5.ipa       (empty placeholder file)

3. No other files are present in /home/user/mobile_ci/reports or /home/user/mobile_ci/tools.

Verification logic the autograder will use after the agent finishes:

• Confirm that /home/user/mobile_ci/tools/generate_build_report.sh exists and is executable.  
• Execute the script in a clean shell; capture STDOUT.  
• Read /home/user/mobile_ci/reports/build_report.csv.  
• Expected literal content for both STDOUT and the CSV file:

    platform,total_builds,latest_version
    android,3,1.2.3
    ios,4,1.2.5

• Any deviation (extra spaces, missing newline, wrong counts, etc.) will fail the test.

No further hidden files, directories or values are involved.
